data.title: Flight to safety: US10Y falls below 4%; eyes on Fed's Powell and payrolls data
data.description:
Markets are still reeling from President Donald Trump's aggressive across-the-board tariffs, which fueled a global stock selloff, wiped out over $2T from Wall Street and reignited concerns of a prolonged trade war.  

Investors rushed to safer assets like bonds, with the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) falling below 4% for the first time since October 2024. At the time of writing, (US10Y) was down 17 bps at 3.88%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 21 bps to 3.52%.

Traders now await Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech on Friday, hoping he'll offer some clues on the U.S. economy and what the tariffs could mean for the central bank's monetary policy.

Also in focus is the March nonfarm payrolls data, which will be released before Powell takes the stage. Economists are expecting a slowdown in jobs growth, and an uptick in unemployment.

Markets are now increasingly expecting the Fed to lower interest rates by at least 100 basis points this year, possibly through four separate 25-bp cuts starting in June. Traders had been factoring in about 75 bps of cuts before the tariffs were announced.

"Fed officials have tried to play it cool recently," said ING's Chris Turner. "Given that the market is now fully pricing a 25-bp June cut, any suggestion that the central bank will not be rushed into rate cuts could hit equities and subsequently weigh on the dollar too."

data.title: Matador Resources sells remaining Eagle Ford assets in deals exceeding $30M
data.description:
Matador Resources (NYSE:MTDR) said Friday it sold its remaining Eagle Ford shale position in south Texas in series of transactions totaling more than $30M over the last two quarters.

Matador (NYSE:MTDR) said it applied a portion of its cash flows and over $30M in proceeds from the sale to reduce debt; in total, the company repaid $180M of borrowings under its credit facility during Q1 and ended the quarter with $405M under the facility, based on a preliminary review.

"Matador is excited to continue its primary focus on developing its high-quality acreage in the northern Delaware Basin," Chairman and CEO Joseph Foran said, adding the company owns ~200K net acres in the basin, 80% held by production.

The company also said it has already secured inventory for the majority of its 2025 drilling program, and does not expect any recent tariffs to impact its well costs until this year's H2.

Matador (MTDR) shares plunged 14.6% on Thursday, falling alongside most other oil and gas stocks in response to President Trump's tariff announcement.

data.title: MarketAxess delivers 31% higher average daily trading volume in Q1
Maritime Resources issues shares for interest payment
data.description:
Maritime Resources (TSXV:MAE:CA) (OTCPK:MRTMF) said that its previously announced contemplated issuance of common shares to holders of $5M non-convertible senior secured notes issued on August 14, 2023.

The company has issued ~2.57M interest shares in satisfaction of the interest payable thereunder as of March 31, 2025 in the aggregate amount of $129,500.

In accordance with the terms of the notes, the company issued the interest shares in satisfaction of the interest payment at a deemed price of C$0.072 per interest share. T

data.title: Commodities take a hit as Trump reciprocal tariffs dampen sentiment, Brent slides over 3%
data.description:
President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs have rocked commodity markets, with almost all sub-asset classes displaying losses and dragging down the Bloomberg Commodity Index.

As the risk of a U.S.-led global recession has risen substantially, BMI expects investor sentiment to tilt towards expectations of a challenging demand outlook for commodities in the coming weeks.

While this would be somewhat mitigated by a weakening U.S. dollar, which is likely to prevent a full collapse in most commodity prices, the brokerage said.

It expects negative sentiment to hurt industrial metals, oil and agricultural grains most, while natural gas and agricultural softs remain supported by supply side factors at the moment.

Oil and gas imports to the U.S. have been exempted from the new round of tariffs. That said, crude markets responded to the tariff announcement by sending prices sharply lower with Brent (CO1:COM) down -3.40% to $67.75 a barrel, and WTI (CL1:COM) -3.64% to $64.52 a barrel by 6 am ET.

ETFs: (XLE), (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Industrial metal prices have also seen some losses following the tariffs, and BMI maintains the view that tariffs will prove bearish for the industrial metals sub-asset class, with the risk of a deceleration in economic growth in major markets posing a major headwind to metals demand.

“Moreover, tariff threats continue to remain in focus for metals, despite current exemptions from reciprocal tariffs, for instance for copper, keeping the markets on edge in the coming weeks.”

ETFs: (CPER), (NYSEARCA:COPX), (OTC:JJCTF)

Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) has meanwhile benefited from escalating trade tensions, reaching an all-time high of $3,168/oz on April 3, and analysts expect bullion to have further room to run.

In softs, amidst the prevailing concerns of U.S. tariffs overshadowing the commodities landscape, the narrative for agricultural softs remains driven by fundamental forces. The U.S. is a major exporter of cotton, and the risk of retaliatory tariffs on U.S. cotton exports looms large.

ETFs: (WEAT), (SOYB), (CORN), (DBA), (NYSEARCA:MOO)

data.title: Filament Health to raise C$0.9M, delist from Cboe Canada
data.description:
Clinical‐stage natural psychedelic drug development company, Filament Health (NEOE:FH:CA) (OTCQB:FLHLF) expects to complete the financing for net proceeds of approximately C$0.9M.

The company also announced that it intends to delist its common shares voluntarily from Cboe Canada, after obtaining necessary shareholder approval.

Pursuant to the financing, the company will issue a unit comprised of a secured convertible debenture of the company and a warrant for the purchase of common shares of the company.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures slide as tariff driven selloff continues; eyes on labor data
data.description:
The gloomy mood on Wall Street is expected to persist, with stock index futures dropping on Friday as President Donald Trump's latest tariffs continue to spook markets. Investors are also awaiting key labor data.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.9%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 9 basis points to 3.96%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 6 basis points to 3.66%.

U.S. stocks were mauled Thursday, with the S&P 500 closing in a correction as President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs stoked fears of a global trade war. The S&P 500 closed at -4.8%, finishing its worst session since June 2020.

The selloff occurred after Trump late Wednesday detailed new tariffs that the U.S. is enacting on imports. The minimum baseline tariff will be 10%.

"The last 24 hours have been truly historic for markets, as the impact of the U.S. reciprocal tariffs cascaded across different asset classes, with no sign of letting up overnight," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"In terms of what happens now, the big question is how the U.S.’s trading partners might retaliate, as that will play a huge role in determining what the overall economic and market impact will be… On the back of all this, investors grew increasingly fearful about a potential US recession, with U.S. equities seeing their sharpest decline in years," Allen added.

Financial markets expected a significant tax increase from Trump. Yesterday’s reaction shows the tax increase was worse than anticipated. U.S. dollar weakness is telling. We often hear that when the U.S. sneezes the global economy catches cold. This is not the US sneezing. This is the U.S. cutting off its own arm, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"For now, US recession risks have risen. Over the longer term, effective tariff rates should fall somewhat. Rerouting supply chains, shifting demand patterns, and the political and economic pressure on Trump to retreat should reverse some of this damage," Donovan added. 

Traders will keep their eyes on the important March jobs report slated to come before the bell. Economists expect job additions to fall to 135K from 151K in February and the unemployment rate to stay level at 4.1%.

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is expected to speak at the SABEW conference in Arlington, Virginia.

data.title: Nvidia takes part in $300M funding round of AI video startup Runway
data.description:
AI video startup Runway AI secured over $300M in funding from investors including Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA).

The New York-based startup said the funding was led by General Atlantic with participation from Fidelity Management & Research Company, Baillie Gifford, Nvidia, SoftBank Vision Fund 2 and other new and existing investors.

The funding values the company at $3 Billion, Bloomberg News reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Runway also announced an expansion of Runway Studios —  its AI film and animation studio dedicated to producing original content using the foundation models made by its research team.

The company noted that the money will help it expand and advance its research in media generation and support in developing new AI research for new media. Runway added that it continues to hire researchers and engineers.

Recently, Runway had unveiled its latest media generation model called Gen-4 which can precisely generate consistent characters, locations and objects across scenes, according to the company.

Runway’s software has been used for projects, including generating some scenes for Amazon's (AMZN) House of David, creating visuals for a Madonna concert tour and producing an ad for Puma, according to the Bloomberg report.

The company has also signed an agreement with Lionsgate to train an AI model on the studio’s content which can be used in its film projects. Runway's Co-Founder and CEO Cris Valenzuela said the company is partnering with more studios to make films with them, but he did not disclose their names, the report added.

data.title: China's Niu Technologies reports 57% increase in Q1 sales
data.description:
• NIU (NASDAQ:NIU) sold 203,313 units in the first quarter of 2025, up 57% from a year ago.
  • The company sold 183,065 units in China and 20,248 units in the international markets.
  • In China, the company achieved sales volume growth of over 60% year-over-year.
  • Furthermore, the launch of the NX Pro in late March received an overwhelmingly positive market reception, generating massive pre-order demand across all sales channels and attracting over 3.5 million viewers during its live debut broadcast, the company said.
